code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-DEC-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and low total CO₂, which may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising concern for renal dysfunction and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, hydration status, and possible further nephrological evaluation.
